---
title: "Historical Trends"
format: html
---

# Scorigami Through the Decades

This page shows how NBA scoring patterns have evolved over the past 80 years, with scorigami visualizations for each decade.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)

# Read dataset
games <- read_csv("Games.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  filter(homeScore > 0, awayScore > 0) %>%
  mutate(year = year(gameDateTimeEst),
         decade = (year %/% 10) * 10)

# Get unique decades and sort descending
decades <- sort(unique(games$decade), decreasing = TRUE)

# Function to create scorigami plot for a decade
create_decade_plot <- function(decade_data, decade_label) {
  score_counts <- decade_data %>%
    count(homeScore, awayScore)
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_tile(data = score_counts, aes(x = homeScore, y = awayScore, fill = n), width = 1, height = 1) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = c("#2c1e50", "#7f1e7f", "#d83c32", "#f58a24", "#ffd13b", "#ffffff"),
      trans = "log10",
      name = "Game Count",
      breaks = c(1, 5, 20, 100, 500),
      labels = c("1", "5", "20", "100", "500+")
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 180, 20), limits = c(15, 190)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(20, 180, 20), limits = c(15, 190)) +
    labs(
      x = "Home Points",
      y = "Visitor Points",
      title = paste(decade_label, "- NBA Scorigami")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.key.height = unit(0.8, "cm"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.2)
    )
}

# Create plots for each decade
plots <- list()
for (d in decades) {
  decade_data <- games %>% filter(decade == d)
  decade_label <- paste0(d, "s")
  plots[[as.character(d)]] <- create_decade_plot(decade_data, decade_label)
}

# Display plots
for (i in seq_along(plots)) {
  print(plots[[i]])
}
```

## Analysis

The evolution of NBA scoring patterns over these decades reveals interesting trends:

- **Scoring ranges**: Earlier decades show more clustering at lower scores, while recent decades have broader scoring distributions
- **Home-court advantage consistency**: The asymmetry below the 45° diagonal (favoring home teams) remains consistent across all decades
- **Game frequency**: The density and frequency of games has increased significantly in recent decades, reflecting the expansion of the league and increased sample sizes

This historical perspective demonstrates that while the fundamental advantage of playing at home has persisted throughout NBA history, the overall scoring landscape has shifted as the game has evolved.
